etimesMan had ten nights of chilling visions showing the deadly crash of Flight 191 that killed 273In May 1979, American Airlines Flight 191 crashed near Chicago, killing 273. Days before, David Booth reported experiencing ten consecutive nights of vivid visions foretelling the disaster. Though he contacted the FAA, authorities lacked actionable details. The visions eerily mirrored the crash, leaving Booth and witnesses haunted. Flight 191’s tragedy remains entwined with unsettling folklore and aviation superstition.

etimesNo pink panthers or green dogs? Why are mammals not as brightly coloured as birds and fishMammals primarily exhibit muted earth tones due to their reliance on melanin and a lack of structural color complexity in fur. This drab coloration evolved as a survival mechanism to hide from predatory dinosaurs. Even after the dinosaurs' extinction, mammals retained these colors, partly due to their dichromatic vision, which limits their perception of vibrant hues.
